description_long: This object is a vintage cathode ray tube (CRT) television, featuring a large, slightly curved glass screen encased in a rectangular housing. The front panel includes two prominent rotary dials for channel and volume control, as well as a speaker grille below the controls. The casing is primarily black with a faux woodgrain finish on the sides, giving it a classic retro appearance. The rear of the unit is bulky, extending outward to accommodate the CRT and internal electronics, with visible ventilation slits and a service panel. The base is flat and sturdy, designed to rest securely on a table or entertainment stand. This type of television was commonly found in homes and public spaces from the mid-20th century through the early 2000s.
description: A vintage CRT television with a curved glass screen, rotary dials, and a black and woodgrain exterior. The back is bulky to house the cathode ray tube and internal components.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large glass screen, two rotary dials, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep, protruding back and woodgrain panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the extended CRT housing, ventilation slits, and service panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the same deep back and woodgrain finish as the other side.
